title: Cyclosorus subpennigerus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus subpennigerus is classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The genus Cyclosorus is a part of the family Thelypteridaceae. Within this family, Cyclosorus subpennigerus is further classified as a species of the genus Cyclosorus. The species is native to certain parts of Asia, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships within the Thelypteridaceae family.

intro: Cyclosorus subpennigerus, also known as the sub-pennated lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. Traditionally, Cyclosorus subpennigerus has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cyclosorus subpennigerus include tea infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat fever, digestive issues, and inflammatory conditions.
